title: Robinhood Stock Is Down 30% in 2026. Here's Why Analysts Still See $125 Fair Value
text: Despite significant year-to-date losses, Wall Street analysts maintain bullish outlooks on Robinhood Markets. Recent Performance: HOOD shares have declined approximately 30-37% in 2026 following disappointing Q1 earnings and broader fintech sector weakness. The stock has been pressured by concerns about retail trading volume sustainability and cryptocurrency market volatility. Analyst Sentiment: Despite the pullback, analyst consensus remains constructive with 36 buy ratings and only 3 hold ratings covering the stock. The median price target is approximately $110, implying 35%+ upside from depressed levels. Growth Drivers Ahead: The SEC's removal of the $25,000 Pattern Day Trader minimum rule is expected to unlock an estimated 25% of funded accounts previously restricted from active trading. This regulatory tailwind takes effect June 4, 2026. Upcoming Catalysts: Q2 earnings metrics will reveal whether the PDT rule elimination boosts trading volumes. Expansion of prediction markets, which grew 320% in Q1. International expansion initiatives in Singapore and other markets. Valuation Perspective: At current depressed levels, the stock is trading at more reasonable multiples relative to growth prospects. Analysts believe the market is excessively punishing the stock for a single weak quarter, particularly given the company's strong fundamentals and upcoming catalysts.
